description: A `switch` in EXPRESSION position — inside an `if=${...}` attribute expression — fires E-SWITCH-FORBIDDEN. Pins the third and structurally distinct fire site: expression position needs its own raw-text guard because a JS expression parser rejects `switch` as not-an-expression, so no switch node ever reaches the statement-loop guards the other two POS cases hit.
rationale: §34 (E-SWITCH-FORBIDDEN, cross-ref §17) bans the keyword with no position carve-out — statement or expression. This case is the anti-regression pin for the SILENT-DROP failure mode: without a dedicated expression-position guard, `switch` in an attribute expression is swallowed by the expression parser's escape hatch and the author gets NO diagnostic at all — the exact soundness hole §34's row forbids. Asserting the code here fixes that the diagnostic reaches the author regardless of syntactic position.
